name: Nobl9
what_it_does: SLO management platform with a REST API for defining, tracking, and reporting Service Level Objectives and error budget burn rates across data sources.
use_cases: Define SLOs in YAML or via API and track error budget consumption in real time
Alert on error budget burn rates before SLOs are breached using configurable thresholds
Query SLO status programmatically to gate deployments or trigger rollbacks in CI/CD pipelines
Report on SLO compliance across multiple services for engineering leadership dashboards
not_for: Teams without existing metrics infrastructure such as Prometheus, Datadog, or Splunk
Simple uptime monitoring needs where a basic synthetic check tool suffices
best_when: Best when reliability engineering teams need a dedicated SLO layer that aggregates data from multiple observability backends.
avoid_when: Avoid when your observability platform (e.g., Datadog, Grafana Cloud) already provides native SLO tracking sufficient for your needs.

auth.notes: Client credentials flow using Client ID and Client Secret generates access tokens. Tokens are scoped to organization. Terraform provider uses the same credentials.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Limited number of SLOs and data source connections on free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Paid plans from approximately $300/month; enterprise pricing on request
pricing.notes: Free tier is suitable for evaluation only. Production use requires a paid plan for meaningful SLO counts.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits not prominently documented; standard SaaS throttling applies

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns descriptive JSON error bodies with field-level validation messages for YAML/config errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: SLO and objective definitions are declarative; applying the same definition twice is idempotent. Mirrors Kubernetes-style apply semantics.
known_agent_gotchas: Data source integrations must be configured before SLOs can reference them; agents cannot create SLOs without pre-existing data sources
Burn rate calculations have a delay depending on data source polling interval
Terraform provider is the most mature programmatic interface; REST API has less coverage than Terraform resources

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow is appropriate for machine-to-machine use. SOC 2 Type II certified. TLS enforced.
